Pros: liked the dress,got alot of compliments,loved the white and the material was ok Cons: I'm 5'1 so it was definitely long on me even with my heels, the splints are really high any lil move and everything was showing Recommendation:make sure you have the proper undergarments

Great last-minute dress Pros: Super flattering, not too long for my petite frame (I’m 5’1” with an athletic build & wearing ~2.5” heels in the picture), stretchy material that is very comfortable and easy to move in, very fast next day shipping Cons: Quality isn’t the best since it’s a thinner jersey material and I found some loose stitching, but I don’t expect high quality from Amazon so I’m fine with it especially as a last minute find

Very long, super dark navy almost black Pros: This dress is beautiful! It fits well, looks fantastic on my 5’4” 138lb body. It’s about 12” too long for me. It also has a nice finish to the back waist band which is stretchy and about 1” in depth. Much better look than other dresses that have just a bit of elastic. It’s a good quality. Weighs about 1.5lbs for those wondering what others mean by heavy. I feel they are talking about the fabric which I felt was nice and not too heavy.Cons: the Navy Blue, which is so dark it looks almost black actually came off on my hands like old newspapers used to do. I quickly put it back in the packaging so it wouldn’t rub off on anything else. Also as I noted above it’s very very long! You would need to shorten it.If the color had worked for me I would have definitely kept this but I’m looking for a different blue.I have taken a picture of it next to black fabric to show difference.

Super Cute and Flattering Pros: This fabric is thick and the dress has a nice lining. The dress has some stretch. I wear normally wear size 8/10 misses, I am 5'1 1/2" or 61.5", and165lbs. I purchased a size Med. Cons: There appears to be a small stain in the ruching area. The armpit whole was slightly smaller than I am used to but, this is made in China. I wonder if I went up a size, would the armpit whole been bigger.

Awesome Dress, Great Value! PROS: This is a great dress! I wore it to my bridal shower and l will probably wear it as a reception dress for my wedding. I ordered a white XS and it fit perfectly (i'm generally between a size 0 and 2, but I would check the chart they provide either way). I love the neckline (you do need a bra that works with this; I had a bra that readjusted to a halter, but I still needed to hook the front straps together--it wasn't too big of a deal, though), the asymmetrical hemline drapes well (I'm only 4'9, but with 3 inch heels, the back of the dress just grazed the floor), the lace is beautiful, the material soft, and the zipper zips without struggle. I got so many compliments--people couldn't believe I bought it online for such a great price! CONS: Nothing...if I have to say something, there was this weird stain in the inside of the dress when I first got it. But it was so tiny and unimportant, it didn't matter. Thought I would just mention for completeness' sake. I wish I had more excuses to wear this dress tbh....buy it!

It’s comfy & beautiful but be careful when taking photos it will snag due to the lace fabric! Purchase this for my maternity photos. Was nervous since I’m plus size & sometimes things don’t fit right but this dress fit perfectly & complimented my figure nicely. It was flowy & long enough (I’m 5’8) & paired well with my husband’s outfit. Very happy I took the plunge & order the dress! Also be mindful if you’re expecting to check to see if the front stays down, sometimes it rose up & I needed to pull the slip down!

Very stiff fabric Purchased (the green floral dress) for a daytime summer wedding. Fabric is too thick (so I ended up returning it). It is also much stiffer/starchier than I expected. Ordered a size M (US 6), which seemed to be a good fit for my frame (5’7”, 145 lbs). All & all, if you don’t mind the stiff fabric, it seems to be a good buy for the price.

It depends . . Purchased a couple sizes and colors. Periwinkle is more like royal (see picture); that color and the dark eggplant looked old-fashioned to me The lighter rose color seems more youthful, and petite would be perfect in the length with short heels (I'm 5' 4.5"), but the dress I tried in 12 regular fits better in the bodice--a little less tight under the arm and the narrowest point hits my waist better. Waiting for a regular 12 to see if it works. I usually take a medium in dresses (10/12). Delivery is spotty. Rose came on a hanger and in a large box folded nicely; others came stuffed in small boxes with one jammed in so the hanger broke, and the other without either a hanger or a tag as the others had.

32 Wks pregnant Size Purchased a size large at 183lbs and 32 weeks pregnant. Pros: Stretches around the stomach, not too tight around the legs/Hip area, does not irritate the skin (it’s not itchy) Cons: the sleeves would continuously fall down or move, had to readjust several times in between photos.the bunching of the fabric stretched out ( I would’ve liked to have a more scrunched looked, as pictured.) Additional tip (Shoes): it’s not pictured but the shoes that ended up fitting my feet were also from Amazon “DREAM PAIRS Women's Square Open Toe Slide Sandals Cute Slip on Braided Strap Rhinestone Flat Sandals for Summer”

Size runs big Purchased a small and it was WAY too big. I’m 5’2 130 lbs and the length was too long (pass my knees) and didn’t look flattering on. Its loose fitting and felt like I was wearing a maternity dress.

Don’t hesitate just buy it! Purchased at the last literal second for a wedding as I wanted to wear something flowy and romantic for late summer and realized that my closet is still stuck in the 2012 bodycon era. Followed the size chart and got the XL and it fits with room to spare, including the underarm part which is elastic and was my main concern as a busty lady. For reference I am 5’7/275 and typically an XXL so this was a VERY pleasant surprise! Fabric is thick and unlined and I can see what some were saying about the pleats being “off” with the belt added but I don’t mind that. My only wish was that the “belt” was a bit longer as I think the double tied look would be more flattering (giving more of the Grecian/Megara effect) but that’s just my personal preference. Will update post wedding should anything else seem , about to buy the black now!

Pleasantly surprised Purchased for a baptism. The dress is beautiful. Many compliments. Very comfortable. Hides anything you don’t want on display lol like a mommy tummy! 5’2 155 lbs. got a medium.

Beautiful quality, little big. Purchased for a fancier dinner while on a family vacation, the quality is AWESOME!! The lace is super soft, and the fabric has a little stretch to it. I’m about 5’2”, 200 pounds and carry my weight in my belly and chest. I’m about a 40DD, did measurements with the provided chart and ordered an 18, and could’ve went with a 16 as the dress is a little big but nothing that I can’t make work. Some reviews that I read say it comes with a button on the bust area, however mine did not, but it covers my chest well. Comes down a little below my knee as well. Would 10/10 recommend only reason for the 4/5 review is because of the fit being slightly off, other wise a fantastic product!

Great Buy! Purchased for a friend’s wedding and it’s my new favorite dress! The fit was true to size and very comfortable. I’m 5’8” and 150 with an hourglass shape. You can easily wear a normal bra under it and the lace top was very flattering without being too revealing. It was also comfortable while dancing and in the Florida heat. Simply lovely! Purchased this dress for our engagement photos. I was little nervous because I’m 5’ 2” and wasn’t sure if it would be too long for me. But looking at the reviews and seeing it on women my height made me feel a bit more confident. And because “Operation: Trim The Fluff” is still ongoing, I’m between sizes. I went with Medium because I didn’t want anything to cling unflatteringly. Being short, I was also worried that I would look like a wine-colored blob, but that wasn’t the case! The wrap bodice is really flattering (especially for someone like me who’s a member of the IBTC). With heels, the length was perfect, IMO. It’s also a great dress for twirling. It moves really well. If you’re getting photos in this, definitely make it move by twirling, flicking the dress, etc. I got soooo many compliments on our engagement photo previews about how it “moved”. I WILL say, the fabric is kind of thin (not see-thru thin, especially since the color is dark), but it won’t keep you warm. It also wrinkles pretty easily. When it arrived, I did a round of steaming to get the folding creases out of it and then steamed it again before the shoot. I’m looking forward to wearing it out on a date night when I want to “look like a girl”.

Cute But Runs Small Really cute dress with a nice drapes to it that gives the dress an elegant look. The fabric is of a nice quality for the price and has a good weight to it. I did find this dress to run a bit small. I ordered the XS and the measurements for the bust should be 33 inches. I only measure 31 inches on a good day, with me dropping to 30.5 most of the time and I am an A cup. This dress was so tight in the bust. I can honestly say I don't think that has ever been a problem for me. The rest of the measurements of this dress are off too. It was definitely too small overall. I ordered the XS and I am 5'10, 118 lbs, 31A-25-34 for reference. With a tulip hem, the fit in the waist and hips HAS TO be pretty much spot on because of the way it is cut and drapes. If you feel the split is way too high and the other side doesn't cover as it should that means it is too small. And when you couple that with a dress that was cut just a bit too small on the under flap, you will end up showing all your goodies. So if you want to take a chance on this dress, size up.

Great quality, reasonable price Really great quality for a reasonable price! For reference, I’m 5’10”, 160lbs, 32C, and got a medium. I was nervous about the top being too big, cause I typically like a secure fit and get a small in things like sports bras. But it actually fits quite well! The top is a just smidge loose for my liking, but it’s not actually falling down, I’ll just have to readjust every now and then. There’s also a strip of rubber at the top to prevent it from going anywhere. The rest of it fits really nicely - not tight at all, and the quality is thicker, silkier, and sturdier than I expected, while still being lightweight and not too warm. It’s a beautiful color but a bit see-through around the lighter parts, however it’s nothing that some nude underwear can’t solve. I did expect the length to be a bit shorter than on the model - it hits a few inches above my ankle since I’m on the taller side. No weird smells, adequately packaged, and it shipped so quickly (bought on a Friday, arrived Monday)! If you’re on the fence, it’s totally worth buying this - it’s really cute, very comfy, and a reasonable price!

Amazon for the win!!! Recently, I was invited to a black-tie event last minute. While I did end up running around to a couple stores and found another option, I thought I’d order this dress as a back-up to see what I thought because the reviews looked so good. Boy - am I glad I did! I lost 40 pounds in the past year and the other option I had found made me feel like an old lady - super covered up. This dress was the perfect elegant balance. The cleavage is covered and it is kind to a bit of a belly and hips. The fabric is forgiving and not clingy nor easily wrinkled. I also appreciated the length. I’m 5’6” and was able to wear 1.5-2” heels and slippers that were given out to walk around in without tripping on the hem of the dress or having it look too short. And can we talk about the price?!! The value for this dress is ridiculous!!! I wish I had occasions to buy it in other colors, let alone wear it more! Overall, I think it’s a very flattering dress that made me feel comfortable and confident for my first time in a dress like this in years!

Amazing! Red is my absolute favorite color! I am pretty picky when it comes to my formal gowns. I was wanted something different and unique. When I saw this dress I read every review before I made my purchase. Let me tell you not only is the color of red pretty but the fit and material was on point. I did not even have to alter it and I am 5’1”. The surprise was the mini train in the back. I just received one compliment after the other. Ladies if you are looking for a formal dress that you want to fit perfect and not alter it in anyway buy this dress!!! I highly recommend it!

I’m 5’7” and normally wear a size 16/XL, but decided on the XXL for this dress as the numbers in the size chart matched mine best. Definitely consult the provided measurements when deciding what suits you because they are accurate in my estimation; there’s no way I would have fit into the 16/XL size.There isn’t much stretch to the lace top as it’s obviously formfitting. A long, hidden zipper down the length of the back helps you get into the dress, but you’ll probably need assistance getting it zipped. The lace sleeves are fairly snug but not constrictive, and the satin ribbon at the waist is sewn into the garment. I like the ruffle of the overlayed skirt, and its asymmetrical effect is pretty, too. Overall, there’s not a single thing I don’t like about this dress—it’s even machine wash (with the use of a laundry back to protect the lacy material) and line dry.

True to size, maybe even too big Size down or stay true to your size, even if you’re between sizes, but DO NOT size up. I had to be sewn in and still had to have the corset back tightened as far as it could go because it was so large on me. Keep in mind, at the time I was 225 lbs. The top was also so large that we had to fold it and pin it in place, even so that the sleeves still slipped through out the night. I’d say this dress is very much well suited for bigger chested women (which I am not). Be ready to have to make major adjustments if you size up. Also if you’re ordering for a big event like I did, don’t wait to order. This took almost 3 weeks to get to me and I was terrified I wasn’t going to get it in time for my event. Over all if I bought again, I would buy my actual size or even size down. 10/10 everyone loved it other wise and I felt like a princess.

Absolutely gorgeous! Sizing: I wear a 18/20 in pants, 3X in shirts, 48 DDD in bra. I was not sure what size would fit best based on the sizing chart so I ordered a 6X and a 7X. The 7X fit loose on top and was too long in length. The 6X fit perfectly. Recommendations: I recommend wearing a push-up bra with a little space or a gap in the chest area because the overlapping in the top chest area will show the bra. I also recommend wearing strap heels or strap wedges, because pointy shoes and closed toe heels close the look too much, in my opinion. I also wore a tummy slimming undergarment, which helps smooth out to look. I wore this dress to a wedding in Sedona Arizona. The temperatures were 80° during the day and 56 during the night I did not have to takeoff anything extra and I was very comfortable during the night. The dress is very well-made. It has stretch to the top portion, and the bottom portion had absolutely no fall out.

I'm 5'9", 160lbs, 36D, and normally wear a size 4 in pants/shorts and medium in tops. I got a small in this dress since it seemed pretty stretchy and it fits like a glove. I ordered another velvet dress that I didn't realize was the same brand. That one I got a medium and the medium fit perfect. It will make you feel like the hottest girl in the room lol!

2.5 stars So first, the positives. The burgundy was a nice, rich color. I’m 5’4” and ordered the size 16. It fit very nicely. Length wise, it was perfect. The skirt was a centimeter or two above the floor and I would not have needed to alter the length if I wore a short pair of heels. The neckline was quite deep and my bra was showing no matter how I tried to fix it. A simple hook closure sewn onto the dress would fix the problem though. The shape of the sleeves was also nice and the silhouette of the dress was surprisingly flattering. The bad: the fabric is not great. I would consider it a grade above Halloween costume velvet. You can see it in how the fabric shines in the light. I am afraid of how it would photograph for a wedding I was considering wearing this to. The dress also came with noticeable creases I could not brush out. I’m not sure if washing it before wearing would fix the issue, but I’d be afraid it would not fix the issue. I am therefore returning the dress. All in all, if you have a modest budget or are in a bind for time, then this dress is a fine option.

Never loved a maxi dress so much in my life!! So I am not a big dress girly. I got this maxi dress because I was planning on making my baby bump a disco ball for Halloween (I ended up wearing the dress and putting some stick-on gems on my face). However I really love this dress, when I took it out of the bag I was surprised that it had some weight to it but it wasn't too heavy. It's a great length, I got a small and I'm 127 pounds and 5'3". I'm also 33 weeks pregnant so my belly definitely stretched the dress and made the grey a little see through. Not enough to where I couldn't go to my SIL's Halloween party, but I could see my tattoos through the dress with my bathroom light on while I was getting ready. Definitely need seamless nude underwear and preferably a nude bra at least with the grey dress since I can't speak for the other colors. I haven't washed it a bunch yet so I don't know how sturdy the dress is, but I know it's definitely versatile for lounging at home or to dress up. I'm looking forward to seeing how it fits and if it's less transparent once I'm no longer pregnant, but I do plan on wearing this dress or getting another color/style of this same dress for my baby shower in a week!

Sheer and a bit wrinkled So I got this top because it appeared to be pretty solid and not sheer; however that’s not the case it is quite sheer and will need a camisole under it to avoid too much exposure. The blouse is very well made and I love the sleeves and how light weight it is, it will be a nice summer time shirt, given that it is so sheer and light. The only disappointment I have is the care instructions it can be machine washed but it needs to be hung to dry. Unfortunately when I did this some of the laundry detergent remained on the shirt and it came out pretty wrinkled. I can use a wet cloth to remove the soap residue but I’m concerned with using an iron because the material is so delicate. I may try a quick tumble in the dryer on low to see if that works. I should also mention that I’m 5’5” and 150lbs and my usual size is a medium, I ordered a medium in this shirt and it fit quite well, so it’s pretty true to size. For the most part with a little TLC I think this blouse will be nice to wear and look good overall.

Perfection! So I normally wear 2X and I am 6 foot 3, but I bought the 3X and I’m glad I did! It fit perfectly! The color was exactly as pictured. The fabric is very thick. SOFT and flattering So I ordered two sizes...small and medium. The small in green, and the medium in burgundy/wine. I am 150 lbs, 5'7", 34" chest (AA/A cup) 28" waist and 41" hip, to give you an idea of fit. The burgundy was OK but I didn't feel like it was terribly flattering. Looked a little baggy even though it technically flowed well. The small (green) worked very well for me and eliminated the vaguely baggy look I'd seen in the wine. Fabric is soft and I don't feel any scratching (a common problem for me when there's ruching/twists like the ones at the wrist and ribcage). Fabric is heavy and a nice weight as well as being very stretchy. It flows well. Like some commenters mentioned, the pockets do create a little extra width in the hip area, but I noticed that effect significantly more in the M than the S. Fabric is fairly warm, though I can't speak to breathability (bought this specifically for a fall event). Definitely recommend!

Unfortunately not flattering So I typically wear a size 12 these days (I had a baby this year, so I’ve been trying to add pieces to fit my new postpartum shape). I’m around 5’6”. This dress just wasn’t flattering when I put it on. I am sure I could have fit in a large, but I got an XL because I prefer my clothes not to be as tight since I had a baby. Anyway, I found the dress to just highlight everything I didn’t want it to highlight. I figured with the ruching on the sides, it would be flattering at the stomach, but it wasn’t. As described, apart from the tiny armhole issue So I'm 6'1 and 175lbs at the moment, 36DD/ 34F, and measurements are 39-30-39. I ordered a size up for me, a 12 in mint, fit everywhere but the armpit/ shoulders. I am broader up top than most and athletic so that may be part of it, but I have never had a dress ride so high in the chest and arms before, and honestly don't have too much girth there so not sure what's up. I have ordered the same size in other styles from this brand without issue, so I think is the style of the top, probably best for shorter torsos/ smaller chests and slim shouldered ladies. Otherwise it's flattering and flowy so I gave 3 stars. Just might be to include an arm measurement in their chart for this one. I did just order another style that's like this but chiffon up top, hopefully one of those fits better, but based on other reviews the 14 in this particular style will have a similar issue, plus be to big for my waist. Had to return and not reorder unfortunately.

Great long flows dress So so comfy! It is quite long, I can’t wear flats (I’m 5’2) but great for almost any occasion, dress it up or down. Ties at the should so you can easily adjust as needed. Wore this to a tennis match in Paris and received many compliments.

My kid absolutely loves this dress So the dress is great quality & beautiful, unfortunately we followed the recommendation from reviews to size up & its to big. And even worse we can’t send it back in time. We already spent an extra $30 to get it here in time. But my kid loves the dress so much she has to make a decision to either wear it as is (we can get it to fit by lacing the corset as tight as possible, but you loose that pretty corset look & there’s still a gap); or try and send it back for the correct size & wear a different dress to prom but have the dress in the right size for future wear. So unless the dress sizing is inconsistent in different sizes, I’d rec getting your normal size. My kid is normally somewhere between an XS & small & usually goes with size small. She’s petite (5.2 & 1/2, 102lbs) but has a larger bust for her size (30D). That’s why we often go a bit larger to make sure the bust isn’t to tight. I really don’t think that would be an issue with this dress bc of the adjustable corset. So consider that when it comes to sizing, you have a lot of wiggle room bc the corset goes below the hips. So quality: im impressed. Usually when faced with an item from multiple sellers it’s kind of a c.rap shoot on whether the quality will be good. Sometimes like with this dress you get impressive quality, other times not. Normally I wouldn’t risk it for an important event, but this dress was the only one that fit my very picky kid’s needs. All the American dresses with quicker shipping time (which would have given us time yo return the dress if it didn’t fit the way we wanted), were either to s.exy, to long, to short, and most were sleeveless or had to deep a v neck. Also most didn’t fit her aesthetic, being to sophisticated or to frou frou. My kid likes clothes that have a bit of a cottagecore vibe. The dresses from China were the only ones that fit what she wanted. This dress was the only one that came in a color she wanted, had the style she wanted, wasn’t super short or super long (if you’re shorter this isn’t a mid length dress, it’s ankle length, which was fine, she just worried about tripping over the dress). So when the dress got here and was so well made & the fabric so nice, we were very relieved. There’s only so much you can trust Amazon reviews. So in conclusion we bought a size 6 but should have gotten a size 4. Maybe even a size 2, but I think a 4 would work. I would def go with the size that normally works best for you, & if possible order well ahead of time so if the fit is wrong you can try and return it for the correct size. But the quality is excellent. $65 + shipping is a great price for this dress. (We paid closer to $100 bc of expedited shipping, but I think it’s worth that much if this is the style of dress you want.) Edit: my kid is so in love with the dress it’s more important to have it in the correct size and it’ll be her new go to fancy dress. It really is lovely and if you don’t need it fast, the price is incredible for the quality and look. So we’re going to return it for a smaller size. Fingers crossed we get the right size. My kid is petite so I’m worried a size 4 will still be a bit big but I also don’t want to buy a to small size. So here’s hoping we get the correct size this time.

Edit: my kid is so in love with the dress it’s more important to have it in the correct size and it’ll be her new go to fancy dress. It really is lovely and if you don’t need it fast, the price is incredible for the quality and look. So we’re going to return it for a smaller size. Fingers crossed we get the right size. My kid is petite so I’m worried a size 4 will still be a bit big but I also don’t want to buy a to small size. So here’s hoping we get the correct size this time.
